why: What it's done
Record Q1 revenue (+52%) on May 7 — and still sold off ("beat and fade"). The $43M LivePerson buyout sparked dilution fears; lawsuits circling. Down ~12% on the month, near lows.
The read

Slow bleed, no buyer conviction — flat momentum reads as exhaustion, not a turn. Best mechanics on your list (28M shares/day, tight spreads, a weekly call fits $100). The setup is what's missing, not the tradeability.
Watch for
A clean bounce off $7.85 with big volume — then it's your prime candidate.
What it's done

A recovery bounce, not a breakout. Messy Q1 (May 11): $92M loss, margins hit after FDA forced winding down compounded semaglutide. But landed a Novo Nordisk deal + raised guidance, so the street's repricing the pivot as survivable.
The read
Extended — already above analyst consensus (~$27), upper half of its range, no fresh catalyst until Q2 (~Aug 10). Chasing it mid-range fights resistance.
Watch for
A clean break of $30. Break = momentum lives; fade at $30 = the bounce is done. (Great liquidity, but ATM calls run >$100 — you'd go slightly OTM.)
What it's done

A sentiment pop: founder Tom Siebel returned as CEO May 8 and bought ~$69M of stock. But preliminary Q4 (June 3) showed revenue down 35% for the year and guided down again — and the stock dipped on it.
The read
A dead-cat bounce in a multi-year downtrend ($30+ in 2023 → $8). The post-earnings dip into the $11.82 ceiling says the squeeze is likely spent.
Watch for
Honestly — pass. You'd be buying into resistance with the catalyst burned and nothing new until Sept 9.
What it's done

Cratered ~30% on a June 2 earnings blowup: EPS −$0.17 vs +$0.02 expected, revenue −26%. Cause: a spike in ad/customer-acquisition costs that halved new-customer growth. Management called full-year visibility "challenging."
The read
A falling knife with an unresolved business problem. Its options trade thin (~1M shares/day) — wide spreads, bad fills even if you're right on direction.
Watch for
Nothing tradeable right now. Revisit around next earnings (~late Aug). Your beauty+AI darling — just not this month.
What it's done

Down ~13% in May, ~60% off its highs. Morgan Stanley downgrade on U.S. share losses; Q4 beat May 20 but couldn't hold because management withheld next-year guidance over tariffs. The $1B rhode/Hailey Bieber deal adds debt + integration risk.
The read
A falling knife with a soft floor at $50 — no confirmed base, bounces faded, no catalyst until Aug 5.
Budget reality
A call here runs $355–$660 — 3–6× your $100. Track-only: watch if $50 holds for a few days on volume, and graduate to it when the account's bigger.
